events.subject: The key questions I asked Lay on Aug 22
events.summary: Watkins writes a follow-up note that preserves the questions she says she raised to Ken Lay on August 22 after Enron's disclosure crisis is already visible.
events.excerpt: Sherron Watkins restates the questions she says she raised to Ken Lay and turns them into a preserved follow-up record.

events.subject: IMPORTANT - READ IMMEDIATELY
events.summary: California crisis trading staff are told to preserve material after an attorney general order, creating a strong branch point for strategy, self-reporting, and escalation choices.
events.excerpt: The California Attorney General preservation order lands on the Portland trading desk and turns the power-crisis conduct into an immediate management problem.

events.subject: Baxter Press Release
events.summary: Cliff Baxter's resignation moves through the executive communications loop and gives the benchmark a governance-driven crisis-communications case.
events.excerpt: The resignation press-release thread captures the internal handling around Cliff Baxter's exit.

events.subject: FW: Third Quarter Review of Northern and Transwestern
events.summary: Third-quarter review material moves through the Causey and Hayslett disclosure lane after Enron's losses become public.
events.excerpt: Third-quarter review material is moving with Andersen involvement during the October 2001 disclosure crunch.

events.subject: EES Pre-holiday Update
events.summary: The EES pre-holiday update shows the year-end loss-shift and coordination pressure around energy-services exposure.
events.excerpt: A year-end EES update goes to a broad risk and trading loop just as reporting pressure is building.

events.subject: Braveheart
events.summary: The Braveheart thread exposes the broadband-valuation and structure path that later fed Enron's accounting credibility problems.
events.excerpt: Braveheart is forwarded through the valuation chain, linking the broadband narrative to a concrete branch point.

events.subject: Latest drafts of communications materials
events.summary: The crisis-management communications draft around Skilling's resignation anchors the trust-collapse branch in August 2001.
events.excerpt: Draft communications materials are circulating on the day Skilling resigns, with the crisis-management folder preserving the branch.

events.subject: PG&E Financial Power Deal
events.summary: The PG&E financial power deal remains the counterparty branch, now paired with the repo-owned credit and bankruptcy arc fixtures.
events.excerpt: The deal note captures the legal and counterparty pressure on the PG&E power structure.
